title: NASA Space Apps Challenge 2025
description: The largest annual global hackathon. Teams of up to 6 compete to solve NASA's real open challenges using publicly available space data. No coding experience strictly required — designers, scientists, storytellers all welcome.
eligibility: Open to all ages, worldwide. Teams of 1–6.
tips: Form a diverse team early. Pick a challenge that excites you. Use NASA's open data sets and APIs. Focus on storytelling your solution, not just the code.

title: Chevening Scholarships 2025/26
description: Chevening is the UK government's global scholarship programme. It offers fully funded awards to study any master's degree at any UK university. Chevening Scholars are future leaders, influencers, and decision-makers.
eligibility: Bangladeshi nationals. Bachelor's degree. Minimum 2 years of work experience. Return to Bangladesh for 2 years after study.
tips: Start your personal statement months in advance. Leadership experience is crucial. Show how you'll contribute to Bangladesh after returning. Apply to 3 universities alongside your Chevening application.

title: ICT Division National Hackathon 2025
description: Annual national hackathon organized by the ICT Division of Bangladesh to find innovative technology solutions for the nation's digital transformation goals under Smart Bangladesh Vision 2041.
eligibility: Bangladeshi citizens. Students and professionals. Teams of 3–5.
tips: Align your solution with Smart Bangladesh priorities. Have a working prototype. Focus on real problems faced by Bangladeshis. Government-aligned use cases score well.

title: YSEALI Academic Fellowship
description: A five-week academic program in the United States for emerging young leaders from Southeast Asia. Focuses on civic engagement, entrepreneurship, governance, and sustainable development.
eligibility: Ages 18–25. ASEAN citizens. Currently enrolled undergraduate or recent graduate. Proficient in English.
tips: Demonstrate civic leadership in your community. Show clear goals for your return. Be specific about how the program connects to your work in Bangladesh.

title: Google Science Fair 2025
description: An online science and technology competition open to students ages 13–18 from around the world. Students submit a research project on any science, technology, engineering, or mathematics topic.
eligibility: Ages 13–18. Any country. Individual or team of up to 3.
tips: Pick a problem that personally matters to you. Document your process thoroughly. Make your presentation video engaging and clear. Judges love projects with real-world impact.

title: Commonwealth Scholarship 2026
description: Funded by the UK Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO), open to citizens of Commonwealth countries for postgraduate study at UK universities at master's and PhD level.
eligibility: Bangladeshi nationals. First-class or upper-second-class bachelor's degree. Cannot already be studying or living in a developed Commonwealth country.
tips: Strong academic record is essential. Your research proposal (for PhD) must be well-structured. Clearly explain how your study will benefit Bangladesh.

title: DAAD Research Grant 2025
description: DAAD offers research grants for doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to conduct long-term research stays at German universities and research institutions.
eligibility: Completed master's or doctoral degree. Above-average academic record. Project agreed with a German host institution.
tips: Contact your German host professor BEFORE applying. Your research plan must be detailed and feasible. Language skills in German are a plus but not required for STEM fields.

title: International Math Olympiad 2026
description: The world's most prestigious mathematics competition for high school students, held annually in a different country. Students represent their national team after qualifying through the Bangladesh Mathematical Olympiad.
eligibility: High school students under age 20. Must qualify through national BdMO selection process.
tips: Start with BdMO (Bangladesh Mathematical Olympiad). Practice past IMO problems daily. Focus on number theory, geometry, combinatorics, and algebra.

title: BASIS National IT Awards 2025
description: National awards recognizing excellence in information technology across categories including software, hardware, digital content, IT-enabled services, and startups.
eligibility: Bangladeshi individuals, companies, and startups. Products must be based in Bangladesh.
tips: Enter the startup category early. Document your impact with numbers. A working product is required. Media visibility from winning is extremely valuable.

title: MIT Solve Global Challenge
description: MIT Solve is a marketplace for social impact innovation. Solvers are entrepreneurs applying technology to address global challenges in health, education, economic prosperity, and environment.
eligibility: Open to all innovators globally. Individual or team. Must have a working solution or strong concept.
tips: Frame your solution around a specific challenge theme. Show evidence your solution works. Partnerships with local organizations strengthen your application.

title: Alibaba Global E-Commerce Competition
description: Annual competition for students and young entrepreneurs to showcase innovative e-commerce solutions. Winners gain access to Alibaba's global ecosystem, mentors, and funding.
eligibility: University students and recent graduates worldwide. Teams of 2–5.
tips: Focus on cross-border e-commerce innovation. Show market research. Use Alibaba platforms as part of your solution where relevant.

title: ADB–JSP Scholarship Program 2026
description: The ADB–Japan Scholarship Program provides scholarships for advanced study to outstanding citizens of ADB's developing member countries at designated academic institutions in Asia and the Pacific.
eligibility: Bangladeshi nationals. Bachelor's degree holder. At least 2 years professional work experience. Under 35 years old.
tips: Apply to ADB partner universities directly. Strong academic record and work experience both matter equally. Focus on development economics, public policy, or related fields.
